betterdays.configuration.allowdaysleep.d:
When true, players are allowed to sleep at all times of day in dimensions controlled by Better Days.
Note: Other mods may override this ability.
betterdays.configuration.baserandomticks: The base random tick speed used by the randomTickEffect time effect.
betterdays.configuration.blacklistdimens:
This setting blacklists the sky rendering for specific dimensions. Like in the Aether if using 
/time set command, the sky jitters. Adding to the blacklist will prevent this behavior.
betterdays.configuration.blockentityeffe: When applied, this effect progresses block entities like furnaces, hoppers, and spawners to match the rate of the current time-speed.
betterdays.configuration.clearweatheronw:
Set to 'true' for the weather to clear when players wake up in the morning as it does in vanilla.
Set to 'false' to force weather to pass naturally. Adds realism when accelerateWeather is enabled.
Note: This setting is ignored if game rule doWeatherCycle is false.
Sets the screen alignment of the bed clock.
betterdays.configuration.clockmargin.des:
Sets the distance between the clock and the edge of the screen.
Unused if clockAlignment is CENTER_CENTER.
betterdays.configuration.clockscale.desc: Sets the scale of the bed clock.
betterdays.configuration.clockscale.name: Clock Scale
betterdays.configuration.dayspeed.descri:
The speed at which time passes during the day.
Day is defined as any time between dayStart (see below) and nightStart (see below) the next day.
This is the RATIO of time passing relative to vanilla.
Vanilla speed: 1.0
betterdays.configuration.dayspeed.name: Day Speed
betterdays.configuration.dayspeedminutes:
An alternative way to set day speed. This setting is mutually exclusive with daySpeed.
If both are set, speedMethod (see above) determines which setting is used.
This setting defines the length of the day in real-world minutes.
Vanilla length: 10.0 minutes
betterdays.configuration.daystart.descri:
The time to start day. This is configurable within the time the sun appears and day starts.
Default: 23500
betterdays.configuration.daystart.name: Day Start Time Tick
betterdays.configuration.displaybedclock: When true, a clock is displayed in the sleep interface.
betterdays.configuration.effects.descrip:
This section defines settings for time effects.
These effects modify various game mechanics to match the current speed of time.
These effects can have a significant impact on performance, especially on servers with many players.
betterdays.configuration.effects.name: Effect Settings
betterdays.configuration.enableinterpola:
Enabling this will allow the setting of an infinite amount of time speeds defined as pairs in the form:
(currentTick, desiredSpeed)
where between each value bezier spline interpolation is performed for smooth transitioning
The two default pairs at 0 and 24000 are necessary as they act as bounds. However 
The time speed value for each of them can be freely modified
betterdays.configuration.enablesleepfeat:
Enables or disables the sleep feature of this mod. Enabling this setting will modify the vanilla sleep functionality
and may conflict with other sleep mods. If disabled, all settings in the sleep section will not apply.
betterdays.configuration.enterbed.descri: This message is sent when a player enters their bed.
betterdays.configuration.enterbed.name: Enter Bed Message Settings
betterdays.configuration.gui.description: This section defines settings for visual elements in the client.
betterdays.configuration.gui.name: GUI
betterdays.configuration.hungereffect.de: When applied, this effect progresses player hunger effects to match the rate of the current time-speed.
betterdays.configuration.interpolatedtim:
These are the pairs that define what speed time should run at, at the specified day/night tick
The two default pairs need to exist, but their time speed values can be modified

The value that determines the smoothing factor in the interpolation algorithm.
A high value will make the interpolation softer and curvier
A low value will make the interpolation closer to a piecewise function
betterdays.configuration.leavebed.descri: This message is sent when a player leaves their bed (without being woken up naturally by morning).
betterdays.configuration.leavebed.name: Leave Bed Message Settings
betterdays.configuration.message.descrip: Formatted message to be sent. Look at documentation in config file for details.
betterdays.configuration.message.name: Message
betterdays.configuration.messages.descri: This section defines settings for notification messages.
betterdays.configuration.messages.name: Message Settings
betterdays.configuration.morning.descrip: This message is sent after a sleep cycle has completed.
betterdays.configuration.morning.name: Morning Message Settings
betterdays.configuration.nightspeed.desc:
The speed at which time passes during the night.
Night is defined as any time between dayStart (see below) and nightStart (see below).
This is the RATIO of time passing relative to vanilla.
Vanilla speed: 1.0
betterdays.configuration.nightspeed.name: Night Speed
betterdays.configuration.nightspeedminut:
An alternative way to set night speed. This setting is mutually exclusive with nightSpeed.
If both are set, speedMethod (see above) determines which setting is used.
This setting defines the length of the night in real-world minutes.
Vanilla length: 10.0 minutes
betterdays.configuration.nightstart.desc:
The time to start night. This is configurable within the time sunset starts and night starts.
Default: 12500
betterdays.configuration.nightstart.name: Night Start Time Tick
betterdays.configuration.potioneffect.de: When applied, this effect progresses potion effects to match the rate of the current time-speed.
betterdays.configuration.preventclockwob: This setting prevents clock wobble when getting in bed by updating the clock's position every tick.
betterdays.configuration.randomtickeffec:
When applied, this effect syncs the random tick speed with the current speed of time, forcing
crop, tree, and grass growth to occur at baseRandomTickSpeed multiplied by the current time-speed.
betterdays.configuration.ratioplayersfor:
The ratio of players in a dimension that must be sleeping to skip to morning.
A value of 1 means all players must be sleeping, 0.5 means half the players must be sleeping, etc.
A value of 0 effectively disables this feature.
Ratio of Players required for Sleep
betterdays.configuration.sleep.descripti:
This section defines settings for sleep feature functionality.
If another sleep mod is installed, it is recommended to disable the sleep feature of Better Days
betterdays.configuration.sleep.name: Sleep Settings
betterdays.configuration.sleepspeedall.d:
The speed at which time passes when all players are sleeping.
Set to -1 to disable this feature (sleepSpeedMax will be used when all players are sleeping).
betterdays.configuration.sleepspeedcurve: This parameter defines the curvature of the interpolation function that translates the sleeping player percentage into time-speed.
betterdays.configuration.sleepspeedmax.d:
## THIS SETTING DEFINES THE SLEEP TIME-SPEED IN SINGLE-PLAYER GAMES ###
The maximum speed at which time passes when all players are sleeping.
A value of 110 is nearly equal to the time it takes to sleep in vanilla.
betterdays.configuration.sleepspeedmin.d: The minimum speed at which time passes when only 1 player is sleeping in a full server.
betterdays.configuration.speedmethod.des:
Determines which method is used to set day and night speed.
RATIO: Uses daySpeed and nightSpeed settings.
MINUTES: Uses daySpeedMinutes and nightSpeedMinutes settings.
REALTIME: Sets day and night to 12 real-world hours each.
betterdays.configuration.target.descript: Sets to whom this message is sent.
betterdays.configuration.target.name: Message Target
betterdays.configuration.time.descriptio:
This section defines settings for time manipulation.
This is where you can set day and night speed independently.
You can also set the time speed to change dynamically throughout the day/night cycle.
betterdays.configuration.time.name: Time Settings
betterdays.configuration.title: Better Days
betterdays.configuration.type.name: Message Type
betterdays.configuration.weathereffect.d: When applied, this effect syncs the passage of weather with the current speed of time.
